Row.Text: Transform the late-game into technological transcendence.

Singularity replaces the Science Victory with a path to technological transcendence. Harvest Silicon, fabricate Chips, build Data Centers and Fusion Reactors, convert your civilization to inorganic life, and construct a Dyson Sphere to achieve victory. Requires Gathering Storm.
Silicon
A strategic resource found in deserts and coastal regions. Required for chip fabrication and the construction of advanced computing infrastructure.
Chips
A manufactured strategic resource produced by Chip Fab buildings in the Industrial Zone. Required for Data Centers and advanced computing infrastructure.
Chip Fab

Industrial Zone building. Fabricates basic Silicon chips for use in computing infrastructure. +2 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production. Produces 1 [ICON_RESOURCE_CHIPS] Chip per turn (requires improved [ICON_RESOURCE_SILICON] Silicon).
Advanced Chip Fab

Industrial Zone building. Produces cutting-edge processor designs using refined Silicon. Requires a Chip Fab. +3 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production, +1 [ICON_SCIENCE] Science. Produces an additional 2 [ICON_RESOURCE_CHIPS] Chips per turn (3 total, requires improved [ICON_RESOURCE_SILICON] Silicon).
Expert Chip Fab

Industrial Zone building. Manufactures chips at scale for domestic and interstellar use. Requires an Advanced Chip Fab. +4 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production, +2 [ICON_SCIENCE] Science. Produces an additional 2 [ICON_RESOURCE_CHIPS] Chips per turn (5 total, requires improved [ICON_RESOURCE_SILICON] Silicon).
AI Dividends
Commercial Hub building. AI-driven trading algorithms generate substantial revenue. +10 [ICON_GOLD] Gold.
Fusion Core
Activates the Fusion Reactor, converting [ICON_RESOURCE_URANIUM] Uranium into 16 [ICON_POWER] Power for this city and nearby cities within 6 tiles. +3 [ICON_SCIENCE] Science.
Fusion Reactor

A district that harnesses nuclear fusion to provide clean, abundant power. One per city. Caution: pillaging or natural disasters cause radiation fallout on surrounding tiles. +2 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production per adjacent Industrial Zone.
+{1_num} [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production from the adjacent {1_Num : plural 1?Industrial Zone; other?Industrial Zones;}
Microreactor

A compact nuclear fission reactor that provides 4 [ICON_POWER] Power to the city. +1 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production, +1 [ICON_SCIENCE] Science. Caution: pillaging or natural disasters cause radiation fallout on this tile.
Data Center

A district dedicated to massive computing infrastructure. Multiple allowed per city. Consumes 1 [ICON_RESOURCE_CHIPS] Chip per turn and requires 10 [ICON_POWER] Power. Costs 10 [ICON_GOLD] Gold maintenance. Yields are disabled without Chips or Power. +5 [ICON_SCIENCE] Science, +3 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production. +3 [ICON_SCIENCE] Science per adjacent Data Center or Campus. +2 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production per adjacent Solar Farm. +3 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production per adjacent Microreactor. +5 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production per adjacent Fusion Reactor.
+{1_num} [ICON_SCIENCE] Science from the adjacent {1_Num : plural 1?Data Center; other?Data Centers;}
+{1_num} [ICON_SCIENCE] Science from the adjacent {1_Num : plural 1?Campus; other?Campuses;}
+{1_num} [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production from the adjacent {1_Num : plural 1?Fusion Reactor; other?Fusion Reactors;}
+{1_num} [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production from the adjacent {1_Num : plural 1?Solar Farm; other?Solar Farms;}
+{1_num} [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production from the adjacent {1_Num : plural 1?Microreactor; other?Microreactors;}
Chip Fabs Idle
Your Chip Fabs are not producing [ICON_RESOURCE_CHIPS] Chips because you have no improved [ICON_RESOURCE_SILICON] Silicon. Build a Mine on a Silicon deposit to resume production.
Data Centers Offline
Your Data Centers are not producing yields because your [ICON_RESOURCE_CHIPS] Chip stockpile is empty. Build Chip Fabs to resume production.
Convert to Inorganic Life
Inorganic Life

Converts this city's population to inorganic life forms. After completion, the city no longer requires [ICON_FOOD] Food to grow. Instead, population grows or shrinks based on surplus [ICON_POWER] Power. Requires Synthetic Technocracy government.
City Converted to Inorganic Life
This city's population has been converted to inorganic life. Growth is now driven by surplus [ICON_POWER] Power instead of [ICON_FOOD] Food.
Conversion Failed
Convert to Inorganic Life requires the Synthetic Technocracy government. Change your government and try again.
Already Converted
This city has already been converted to inorganic life.
Inorganic Life
This city has been converted to inorganic life. Population growth is driven by surplus [ICON_POWER] Power instead of [ICON_FOOD] Food.
Inorganic City: population grows from surplus [ICON_POWER] Power instead of [ICON_FOOD] Food. Ensure your city generates enough Power to sustain growth.
Build Dyson Swarm
Dyson Swarm
Design and manufacture the components for a Dyson Swarm — a vast array of solar collectors that will envelop the Sun. Once complete, four launches are required to deploy the swarm in orbit.
Launch Dyson Swarm: Quarter 1
Dyson Q1
Launch the first quarter of the Dyson Swarm solar collector array into orbit around the Sun.
Launch Dyson Swarm: Quarter 2
Dyson Q2
Launch the second quarter of the Dyson Swarm solar collector array into orbit around the Sun.
Launch Dyson Swarm: Quarter 3
Dyson Q3
Launch the third quarter of the Dyson Swarm solar collector array into orbit around the Sun.
Launch Dyson Swarm: Quarter 4
Dyson Q4
Launch the fourth and final quarter of the Dyson Swarm solar collector array into orbit around the Sun.
Complete Dyson Swarm
Complete Dyson
Activate the fully deployed Dyson Swarm and harness the energy of the Sun. Your civilization achieves technological transcendence.
Dyson Swarm Power
This city receives power from the Dyson Swarm orbiting the Sun.
Build Dyson Swarm
Complete Dyson Swarm
Dyson Swarm has not been completed.
Dyson Swarm will be completed in one turn.
